item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Bravo.
Loved this. The ingenuity and imagination in all of it, the tying in of multiple articles. Brilliant.
Was just wanting to see if anybody else had done a skip about Pan and came across this.
I tip my hat to you!!!

item.title: upvote
item.content:encoded: I love the verdant and veldt stuff I've read before and I love this too

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think you drastically misunderstood the point of the piece. The piece was never about the war between the Verdant and the Veldt, at least not primarily. It's at its heart a character piece, and the article really focuses more on the tragedy of what is being done to Miller, rather than the action-packed battles. The war the foundation is fighting is a backdrop and an indirect driver of conflict. If I've led you to expect an action-packed scene, that's on me, and if you can point me to where I've been leading you to that, I'll fix it, but I tried my best to make it clear that this wasn't ever going to be the next 1730.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, "anticlimactic" sounds about right. I was kinda expecting an epic, 1730-esque action scene where Pan-Cernunnos gets taken down, but here I flipped the page, and bam, it's already been defeated. I do like this as the conclusion to the 3240 series (which is why I don't mind this not being a standalone article), and I like Miller's characterization and development. Also appreciated the cameo of the kid from 3240.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this, but I really feel like the ending is a bit anticlimactic, as others have expressed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked the characterization of Miller but the ending feels really anticlimactic. Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just wanted to mention, there's a "SCP-XXXX-1" in the October 30 journal entry. I assume you missed replacing it with SCP-4689.

Not finished reading but loving it so far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You could definitely do more to ease readers into this if they haven't read the previous article- it hits you with a lot of esoteric vocabulary right up front and is supremely technocratic in terms of language construction throughout the description. That being said, the logs do an excellent job of making Miller into a character we can empathize with. Strong characterization in the second half saves an article with a description that requires re-reading and re-re-re-reading in order to follow what's going on. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
hell yeah, critiquing this was a great joy

im glad its on the mainsite now, instant fat +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love all the concepts at play here, love the story, love the writing.

I'll definitely get involved in the Verdant/Veldt dynamic in a future article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, here it is - the sequel to 3240, (which you should read if you want this to make ANY sense) and the conclusion of Agent Miller's story. Thanks to everyone who critiqued me in chat, and to edna, who helped me with the nightmare that is listpages.

If you're interested in writing about the Verdant/Veldt, please shoot me a PM here or over chat and I'd be thrilled to discuss it with you - let's maybe make this a canon!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: EDIT: sorry for stealing the author post spot, but now you’ve fixed the listpages it’s a big fat +1. I loved the draft and love the article
